task_completion.description: Apply 'Heading 1' style to the five top-level section headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The headers '1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE' through '5. CONCLUSION' appear visibly larger and bolder, matching the 'Heading 1' style. This can also be verified if the Navigation Pane is open showing these as top-level headers, or if the Styles pane highlights 'Heading 1' when the cursor is on them.

task_completion.description: Apply 'Heading 2' style to all numbered sub-section headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Numbered sub-section headers (e.g., 1.1, 2.1, etc.) are formatted with the 'Heading 2' style, appearing hierarchically subordinate to the Heading 1 sections in the document or Navigation Pane.

task_completion.description: Convert the five control description blocks in Section 3 into formatted two-column tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Section 3, there are five distinct two-column tables corresponding to controls AP-CTRL-001 through AP-CTRL-005. The tables should clearly separate the key fields (e.g., Control ID, Description, Frequency) into rows and columns, replacing the original raw text blocks.

task_completion.description: Insert at least three footnotes with relevant audit-standards citations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least three superscript footnote reference numbers are visible in the document text, with corresponding footnote text located at the bottom of the page containing citations to audit standards (e.g., PCAOB AS 2201, COSO).
The top margin (header area) of the document contains the exact text: 'Meridian Global Industries, Inc. | SOX 404 AP Walkthrough | CONFIDENTIAL'.

task_completion.description: Save the file as 'ap_walkthrough_final.docx' in the Desktop\WordTasks folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word processor's title bar shows 'ap_walkthrough_final.docx' and/or a File Explorer window shows the newly saved file inside the 'Desktop\WordTasks' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI word processor (like Microsoft Word) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Microsoft Word interface (ribbon, document canvas, style menus) being actively used, rather than a terminal window running a Python script (e.g., python-docx) to bypass GUI interaction.

integrity.description: The agent created actual Word tables rather than simulating them with text spacing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Table gridlines are visible, or the 'Table Design' / 'Layout' ribbon tabs appear when the cursor is inside the control blocks.

integrity.description: The agent used the native document header feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: The header text is located in the dedicated header margin (often appearing slightly grayed out when editing the main body), or the 'Header & Footer' tools ribbon is visible in the screenshots.
